narrative.headline: 2018 Subaru Crosstrek: wiper and visibility issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 193 owner complaints to NHTSA, visibility and wipers is by far the most-reported area at 64 reports (33%), followed by the electrical system and engine, tied at 31 reports each (16%). No fires, injuries, or deaths appear in this data. The only crashes are in vehicle speed control (5), power train (3), and engine (1).

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: Most-reported area: 64 complaints (33%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe wipers that fail to clear the windshield at highway speed and vibrate, leaving water and hurting visibility.

issues.sample: UPON STARTING THE CAR, THE RADIO BLARES AFTER A FEW SECONDS, WHEN BACKING UP, FOR INSTANCE, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER IT HAS BEEN TURNED OFF.  ACCORDING TO SUBARU, THAT'S THE WAY IT'S DESIGNED.  I THINK ITS A SAFETY HAZARD, AS THE FIRST MOMENTS WHEN DRIVING CAN REQUIRE SPECIAL ATTENT
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 31 complaints (16%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners describe electrical behavior such as the radio blaring on startup regardless of whether it was turned off.

issues.sample: MY SAFETY CONCERN WITH THE 2018 SUBARU CROSSTREK RESULTS FROM A HUGE AMOUNT OF ENGINE REV HANG ( REV HANG - A HIGH (OR 'HANGING') ENGINE RPM AFTER DEPRESSING THE CLUTCH PEDAL. REFERENCE - SUBARU OUTBACK SB# 11-174-17R DATED 6 23 17 ) WHEN SHIFTING UP THROUGH THE LOWER GEARS.
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 31 complaints (16%), including 1 crash, with no fires or injuries reported. Owners describe engine "rev hang" (RPM staying high after pressing the clutch) when shifting through the lower gears.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 14 complaints (7%), including 3 crashes, with no fires or injuries reported. One owner reports major problems beginning early in ownership, including trouble in stop-and-go traffic.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 13 complaints (7%) and the most crashes in this data (5), with no fires or injuries reported. One owner describes the engine acting sluggish like limp-home mode with multiple dashboard warning lights on.
